instructions:
You are connected to the 'ntfy-stats' host via rrsh, a read-only diagnostic gateway. The PRIMARY USE of this host is querying Prometheus. The `list_commands` response you just received includes the full allowlist in its `commands` field - each rule's `command` is a list of regexes (element 0 matches the binary path, elements 1..N-1 match the request's `command` elements 1-for-1). The request's `command` length must equal the rule's `command` length AND each element must match its corresponding pattern. Multiple rules can share command[0] to describe alternative command shapes. A rule with empty 'as' authorizes only the SSH user. A non-empty 'as' is a literal allowlist of target users; for any user other than the SSH user (e.g. 'root') you MUST pass {"as":"root"} (or whichever user) explicitly on the run_command call - rrsh does not auto-elevate.

PROMETHEUS HTTP API
  Prometheus runs locally on port 9090. Use /usr/bin/curl -sS against http://localhost:9090/...

  Key endpoints (all GET; URL-encode the PromQL):
    /api/v1/query?query=<promql>[&time=<ts>]                                   instant query
    /api/v1/query_range?query=<promql>&start=<ts>&end=<ts>&step=<dur>          range query
    /api/v1/series?match[]=<selector>[&start=<ts>&end=<ts>]                    series by selector
    /api/v1/labels                                                             all label names
    /api/v1/label/<name>/values                                                values for one label
    /api/v1/targets[?state=active|dropped|any]                                 scrape target health
    /api/v1/targets/metadata?match_target=<selector>                           target-level metric metadata
    /api/v1/metadata[?metric=<name>&limit=<n>]                                 metric type/help metadata
    /api/v1/alerts                                                             active alerts
    /api/v1/rules[?type=alert|record]                                          alerting + recording rules
    /api/v1/status/{config,runtimeinfo,buildinfo,tsdb,flags,walreplay}         server status
    /api/v1/query_exemplars?query=<promql>&start=<ts>&end=<ts>                 exemplars (trace links)
    /api/v1/format_query?query=<promql>                                        canonicalize PromQL
    /-/healthy, /-/ready                                                       liveness/readiness
    /metrics                                                                   Prometheus's own metrics

  Timestamps: RFC3339 (2026-05-21T12:00:00Z) or unix-seconds (1747828800). Step: a duration like 15s, 1m, 1h.

  Pretty-print the JSON by piping to jq:
    pipeline=[{command:["/usr/bin/curl","-sS","http://localhost:9090/api/v1/targets"]},{command:["/usr/bin/jq","."]}]

  Common queries:
    up                                                process up/down per target
    up{job="ntfy"}                                  ntfy-only
    rate(ntfy_messages_published_total[5m])           message-publish rate, 5m window
    histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le)(rate(http_request_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])))   p95 HTTP latency

ALERTMANAGER (port 9093)
  /api/v2/status, /api/v2/alerts, /api/v2/alerts/groups, /api/v2/silences, /api/v2/receivers, /-/healthy, /-/ready, /metrics.

GRAFANA (port 3000, requires Bearer token)
  Set `Authorization: Bearer <glsa_...>` from CLAUDE.md. Read endpoints (GET): /api/health, /api/search?query=, /api/dashboards/uid/<uid>, /api/folders[/<uid>], /api/datasources[/uid/<uid>], /api/annotations?from=&to=, /api/org, /api/user. Query (POST /api/ds/query): body is {"queries":[{"refId":"A","datasource":{"uid":"<uid>"},"expr":"<promql>"}]} - get the datasource uid from /api/datasources first.

OTHER EXPORTERS
  Any exporter on localhost:9XXX exposes /metrics in Prometheus text format. Useful: 9100 = node_exporter (host CPU/mem/disk/net), 9091 = pushgateway. Grep the text for individual gauges, or query Prometheus for time series.

LOGS
  /var/log/prometheus/*.log, /var/log/alertmanager/*.log, /var/log/syslog, /var/log/auth.log. Use journalctl -u <unit> for the in-memory journal (units: prometheus, alertmanager, prometheus-node-exporter).

No write access. /-/reload, /-/quit, and the /api/v1/admin/* endpoints are intentionally NOT in the allowlist. If a command you want isn't allowed, stop and tell the operator rather than trying to work around the matcher.

commands.description: Show the effective username.
Show the user/group identity (uid, gid, groups). No args.
Print the system hostname.
Show how long the system has been up and the load average.
Print kernel/OS info. Only `-a` is permitted.
List processes (BSD-style: ps aux).
List processes (System V-style: ps -ef).
Take one batch-mode snapshot of top.
Report memory usage. Optional unit flag: -h, -m, or -g.
Report filesystem space usage. Optional flag: -h (human-readable) or -i (inodes).
List block devices.
Show network state: addr, route, or link. Accepts any standard `ip` binary path.
List listening sockets with PIDs. Allowed flag combos: -tlnp, -ulnp, -tulnp.

PromQL endpoints that take a `?query=...` URL. Covers /api/v1/query (instant), /api/v1/query_range (range, needs start/end/step), /api/v1/query_exemplars (exemplars, needs start/end), and /api/v1/queries. URL-encode the PromQL: spaces=%20, [=%5B, ]=%5D, etc.
Canonicalize a PromQL expression. Useful for de-obfuscating operator-written queries before reading them.
List series matching one or more `?match[]=<selector>` filters.
List all label names. Optional `?start=&end=&match[]=` filters.
List values for a single label name. Optional `?start=&end=&match[]=` filters.
Scrape target health (/api/v1/targets, optional ?state=active|dropped|any) or target-level metric metadata (/api/v1/targets/metadata?match_target=).
Metric type/help metadata. Optional `?metric=<name>&limit=<n>`.
List currently active alerts.
List alerting and recording rules. Optional `?type=alert` or `?type=record`.
Prometheus server status: live config, runtime info, build info, TSDB stats, command-line flags, or WAL replay progress.
Prometheus liveness/readiness probes. Returns 200 OK or non-2xx.
Alertmanager v2 API: status, active alerts, grouped alerts, silences, receivers. Optional query string for filters.

Read Grafana state via the HTTP API: search, dashboards (by uid), folders, datasources, annotations, tags, health, org/user info. Bearer token must be a Grafana service-account token (glsa_*); use the token from CLAUDE.md.

POST a datasource query to Grafana (run a PromQL query through a Grafana datasource). The -d body is the standard /api/ds/query JSON shape: {"queries":[{"refId":"A","datasource":{"uid":"..."},"expr":"..."}]}. Use the token from CLAUDE.md.
Alertmanager liveness/readiness probes.
Scrape /metrics from any Prometheus exporter on localhost (port 9000-9999). Returns Prometheus text format. Common ports: 9090 Prometheus, 9091 pushgateway, 9093 alertmanager, 9100 node_exporter.
Process JSON. Pipe a curl response into this stage to extract or pretty-print fields. Filter examples: `.`, `.data.result`, `.[0].labels.instance`.
jq with a single flag: -r raw output, -c compact, -S sort-keys, -n null-input, -e set exit code on null/false.
Case-insensitive grep, intended as a pipeline stage (filter Prometheus text-format /metrics output).
Case-insensitive extended-regex grep, intended as a pipeline stage (filter Prometheus text-format /metrics output).
Count lines, as a pipeline stage.
Inspect a stats-stack systemd unit's runtime state.
List systemd timers.
Show the full systemd journal for a stats-stack unit.
Show the last N journal entries for a stats-stack unit.
Show journal entries since a time expression (e.g. '1 hour ago').
Filter a unit's journal by case-sensitive regex.
Filter the last N journal entries by regex.
Tail the last N lines of a Prometheus or Alertmanager log file.
Tail the last N lines of /var/log/syslog or /var/log/auth.log (or their .N rotations).
Read the first N lines of a Prometheus or Alertmanager log file.
Dump a Prometheus or Alertmanager log file. For .gz rotations use zcat.
Read a Prometheus config file under /etc/prometheus (prometheus.yml, alerts.yml, etc.).
Read an Alertmanager config file under /etc/alertmanager.
Dump a gzipped Prometheus/Alertmanager log rotation.
Case-insensitive grep against a gzipped log rotation.
List /var/log/prometheus or /var/log/alertmanager (or a subpath).
Summarize disk usage under /var/lib/prometheus (TSDB) or /var/lib/alertmanager. Recursive on the TSDB can take a while; up to 60s.
Run an instant PromQL query via promtool (alternative to curl + /api/v1/query). Promtool ships with the prometheus package.
Run a range PromQL query via promtool. --start and --end accept RFC3339 or unix epoch; --step is a duration like 15s, 1m.
Validate a Prometheus config or rules file.
